blueprint.title: Automated Pre-Authorization and Specialist Referral Orchestration for Multi-Specialty Clinics

personas.name: Prior Authorization Specialist at a 12-physician Multi-Specialty Clinic
personas.description: Responsible for assembling and submitting pre-auth packets to commercial and government payers, tracking approval status, and resolving denials across cardiology, orthopedics, and neurology service lines.
goals: Reduce average pre-auth turnaround time from 4 days to under 24 hours
Eliminate manual copy-paste of clinical notes into payer portals
Maintain a real-time dashboard of all pending and approved authorizations
pain_points: Manually extracting diagnosis codes, procedure codes, and clinical justification from EHR notes takes 45-90 minutes per case
Payer-specific documentation requirements differ and change frequently, causing rework
No automated alerts when payer decisions are posted, leading to delayed care scheduling

personas.name: Referral Coordinator at a Multi-Specialty Ambulatory Practice
personas.description: Manages inbound and outbound specialist referrals, confirms insurance eligibility, schedules appointments contingent on authorization approval, and communicates status to referring PCPs and patients.
goals: Schedule specialist appointments within 48 hours of authorization approval
Reduce patient no-shows caused by authorization confusion
Provide referring PCPs with real-time referral status updates
pain_points: Referrals sit in a shared inbox with no prioritization, causing high-urgency cases to be missed
Manually calling payers to verify authorization status consumes 2-3 hours per day
Patients frequently call the front desk asking for status updates that the coordinator cannot quickly retrieve

personas.name: Orthopedic Surgeon and Department Medical Director at a Multi-Specialty Clinic
personas.description: Performs high-volume elective and urgent orthopedic procedures that frequently require pre-authorization. Needs clinical documentation to be accurately represented in auth requests without adding administrative burden to the care team.
goals: Ensure pre-auth submissions accurately reflect clinical necessity documented in visit notes
Reduce surgery schedule disruptions caused by last-minute authorization denials
Minimize time spent by clinical staff on administrative authorization tasks
pain_points: Auth denials often cite insufficient clinical documentation that was actually present in the EHR but not included in the submission
Surgery scheduling is blocked when auth status is unknown days before the procedure date
Clinical staff are pulled away from patient care to answer payer peer-to-peer review calls

scenarios.title: End-to-End Pre-Authorization Packet Assembly and Payer Submission for an Elective Orthopedic Procedure
scenarios.description: An orthopedic surgeon orders an elective total knee arthroplasty. The system automatically detects the order, extracts all required clinical evidence from the EHR, assembles a payer-specific pre-auth packet, submits it electronically, and notifies the prior authorization specialist of the outcome.
steps: Orthopedic surgeon finalizes the procedure order for total knee arthroplasty in the EHR, triggering an automated detection event.
EHR Data Extraction Agent pulls relevant clinical notes, imaging reports, conservative treatment history, ICD-10 and CPT codes, and patient insurance details from the EHR.
Payer Policy Compliance Agent retrieves the current prior authorization requirements for the identified payer and procedure code combination.
Pre-Auth Packet Assembly Agent constructs a structured, payer-formatted authorization request with clinical justification narratives mapped to payer medical necessity criteria.
Payer Submission Agent electronically submits the packet via the payer's API or clearinghouse and logs a submission confirmation number.
Authorization Status Monitor Agent polls the payer portal for a decision and sends an in-platform alert to the Prior Authorization Specialist when approval or denial is received.
If denied, the Denial Resolution Agent generates a draft peer-to-peer review request and appeal letter pre-populated with the missing or disputed clinical evidence.

scenarios.title: Referral Triage, Authorization Linkage, and Specialist Appointment Scheduling
scenarios.description: A PCP submits an urgent cardiology referral for a patient with new-onset chest pain. The system triages the referral by clinical urgency, verifies insurance eligibility, links the referral to an active authorization, and schedules the specialist appointment once authorization is confirmed.
steps: PCP submits a cardiology referral order in the EHR with a clinical reason of new-onset exertional chest pain and abnormal EKG findings.
Referral Triage Agent classifies the referral as high urgency based on clinical keywords and flags it at the top of the Referral Coordinator's worklist.
EHR Data Extraction Agent retrieves patient demographics, insurance member ID, and relevant clinical history to support both eligibility verification and pre-auth submission.
Payer Policy Compliance Agent confirms whether a cardiology consultation for this indication requires prior authorization under the patient's specific plan.
Pre-Auth Packet Assembly Agent and Payer Submission Agent assemble and submit the authorization request in parallel with the Referral Coordinator identifying available cardiologist appointment slots.
Authorization Status Monitor Agent detects payer approval and automatically triggers the Referral Coordinator's scheduling workflow with the confirmed authorization number pre-populated.
Referral Coordinator books the specialist appointment, and the system sends automated status notifications to the referring PCP and the patient via their preferred communication channel.

agents.name: EHR Clinical Data Extraction Agent
agents.role: Extracts and structures relevant clinical data from EHR records to support authorization and referral workflows
responsibilities: Parse physician notes, problem lists, imaging reports, lab results, and medication histories from the EHR
Identify and map diagnosis codes (ICD-10) and procedure codes (CPT/HCPCS) associated with the order
Extract conservative treatment history and prior authorization numbers for continuity of care documentation
Normalize extracted data into a structured clinical summary object for downstream agents
inputs: EHR procedure or referral order event
Patient encounter notes and clinical documentation
outputs: Structured clinical summary with ICD-10 and CPT codes
Patient insurance member ID and plan details

agents.name: Payer Policy Compliance Agent
agents.role: Retrieves and interprets current payer-specific prior authorization requirements and medical necessity criteria
responsibilities: Query an up-to-date payer policy database indexed by payer ID, procedure code, and plan type
Identify required clinical documentation elements for the specific authorization request
Flag any recent policy changes that differ from the last submission for the same payer-procedure pair
Return a structured checklist of required documentation fields to the Packet Assembly Agent
inputs: Payer ID and plan type from patient insurance record
CPT and ICD-10 codes from the clinical summary
outputs: Authorization requirement flag indicating whether auth is required for this payer-procedure combination

agents.name: Pre-Authorization Packet Assembly Agent
agents.role: Constructs complete, payer-formatted prior authorization request packets from clinical data and policy requirements
responsibilities: Map extracted clinical evidence to payer medical necessity criteria fields
Generate a clinical justification narrative using structured clinical data and approved language templates
Populate payer-specific forms or API request schemas with all required fields
Perform a completeness check against the payer requirements checklist before handoff to the Submission Agent
Flag any missing documentation gaps back to the Prior Authorization Specialist for manual resolution
inputs: Structured clinical summary from EHR Extraction Agent
Payer documentation requirements checklist from Policy Compliance Agent
Physician attestation or addendum if requested
outputs: Complete payer-formatted prior authorization request packet
Completeness validation report with any identified gaps

agents.name: Payer Submission and Tracking Agent
agents.role: Submits prior authorization requests to payers via electronic channels and maintains submission audit records
responsibilities: Submit completed auth packets via payer API, clearinghouse EDI transaction, or web portal automation
Capture and store submission confirmation numbers and timestamps
Retry failed submissions with exponential backoff and alert the specialist after three consecutive failures
Maintain a submission audit log linked to the patient encounter and order record in the EHR
inputs: Completed pre-authorization packet from Assembly Agent
Payer API credentials and endpoint configuration
outputs: Submission status record written back to EHR and internal dashboard
Failure alert with error details if submission is unsuccessful

agents.name: Authorization Status Monitor Agent
agents.role: Continuously polls payer systems for authorization decisions and triggers downstream scheduling or denial workflows
responsibilities: Poll payer portals and APIs on a configurable interval for pending authorization decisions
Parse approval, denial, and pending additional information responses from payer systems
Push real-time status updates to the Prior Authorization Specialist and Referral Coordinator dashboards
Trigger the scheduling workflow upon approval or the Denial Resolution Agent upon denial
Send automated patient and PCP notifications when authorization status changes
inputs: Submission confirmation number and payer endpoint from Submission Agent
outputs: Authorization decision record with approval number or denial reason code
Automated notification to Referral Coordinator, PCP, and patient

agents.name: Referral Triage and Denial Resolution Agent
agents.role: Prioritizes inbound referrals by clinical urgency and generates appeal documentation for denied authorizations
responsibilities: Analyze referral clinical reason text and diagnosis codes to assign urgency tier using clinical triage logic
Reorder the Referral Coordinator worklist by urgency tier and estimated authorization lead time
On denial receipt, identify the specific denial reason code and map it to missing or disputed clinical evidence in the EHR
Draft a peer-to-peer review request letter and formal appeal with supporting clinical citations
Route the draft appeal to the ordering physician for review and attestation before resubmission
inputs: Referral order with clinical reason and diagnosis codes
Authorization denial record with reason codes from Status Monitor Agent
Original clinical summary and pre-auth packet from Assembly Agent
outputs: Draft authorization appeal with clinical evidence citations
Physician review task routed to ordering provider
